questions.q: Which space agency launched the Chandrayaan-2 mission?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 was developed and launched by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: Chandrayaan-2 was a mission to which celestial body?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2, like the other Chandrayaan missions, was a lunar (Moon) mission.

questions.q: In which year was Chandrayaan-2 launched?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 was launched in July 2019.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-2 consisted of three main components. Which set correctly lists them?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 comprised an orbiter, a lander and a rover.

questions.q: What was the name of the Chandrayaan-2 lander?
questions.explanation: The lander was named Vikram, after Vikram Sarabhai, the father of the Indian space programme.

questions.q: What was the name of the Chandrayaan-2 rover?
questions.explanation: The rover was named Pragyan, a Sanskrit word meaning 'wisdom'.

questions.q: The Chandrayaan-2 lander was named 'Vikram' in honour of which person?
questions.explanation: The lander honours Vikram Sarabhai, widely regarded as the father of the Indian space programme.

questions.q: Which region of the Moon was Chandrayaan-2's landing targeted near?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 aimed to land near the Moon's south polar region, an unexplored area of scientific interest.

questions.q: What ultimately happened to the Vikram lander of Chandrayaan-2?
options: It landed safely and deployed the rover
It crashed during its attempted landing
It never separated from the orbiter
questions.explanation: Vikram lost communication and crashed during its descent, so it did not survive touchdown.

questions.q: Which part of Chandrayaan-2 continued to operate successfully around the Moon after the landing attempt?
questions.explanation: The orbiter continued functioning and studying the Moon from lunar orbit.

questions.q: Which launch vehicle carried Chandrayaan-2 into space?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 was launched on the GSLV Mk III (now called LVM3), India's most powerful rocket at the time.

questions.q: From which spaceport was Chandrayaan-2 launched?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 lifted off from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre at Sriharikota, Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: Who was the Chairman of ISRO during the Chandrayaan-2 mission in 2019?
questions.explanation: K. Sivan was the ISRO Chairman at the time of the Chandrayaan-2 launch and landing attempt in 2019.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-2 was the second lunar mission in the series. Which mission preceded it?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1, launched in 2008, was India's first lunar mission and the predecessor of Chandrayaan-2.

questions.q: What was a key scientific objective of Chandrayaan-2?
options: Returning lunar soil samples to Earth
Studying lunar water and surface composition, especially near the south pole
questions.explanation: A central goal was to study the Moon's surface, mineralogy and water signatures, focusing on the south polar region.

questions.q: Roughly how heavy was the Pragyan rover carried by Chandrayaan-2?
questions.explanation: The small Pragyan rover weighed about 27 kg and was designed to operate near the landing site.

questions.q: How was the Pragyan rover intended to move across the lunar surface?
questions.explanation: Pragyan was a six-wheeled rover designed to roll across the lunar surface.

questions.q: During which month of 2019 did the Vikram lander attempt its landing on the Moon?
questions.explanation: The Vikram lander attempted its descent in the early hours of 7 September 2019.

questions.q: What was the primary power source for the Chandrayaan-2 orbiter and rover?
questions.explanation: Like most ISRO spacecraft, Chandrayaan-2's orbiter and rover were powered by solar panels.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-2 was notable as the first Indian mission to attempt what?
options: A soft landing on the Moon
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 was India's first attempt at a soft landing on the lunar surface.

questions.q: The Chandrayaan-2 orbiter carried a high-resolution camera. Why was this significant?
options: It was the first colour camera flown to the Moon
It was among the highest-resolution cameras ever sent to lunar orbit
It could see through the Moon's crust
It photographed only the Earth, not the Moon
questions.explanation: The orbiter's Orbiter High Resolution Camera (OHRC) was one of the highest-resolution cameras ever sent to lunar orbit.

questions.q: What does the word 'Pragyan', the name of the rover, mean?
questions.explanation: 'Pragyan' is a Sanskrit word meaning 'wisdom'.

questions.q: On what date in 2019 was Chandrayaan-2 launched?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 was launched on 22 July 2019 aboard the GSLV Mk III.

questions.q: At approximately what altitude above the lunar surface did the Vikram lander lose contact during descent?
questions.explanation: Vikram lost communication when it was roughly 2 kilometres above the lunar surface during its final descent.

questions.q: Which planned design life was set for the Chandrayaan-2 orbiter at launch?
questions.explanation: The orbiter was designed for a mission life of about one year, though it carried fuel to operate much longer.

questions.q: How did Chandrayaan-2 reach the Moon to conserve fuel rather than going on a direct trajectory?
options: By gradually raising its Earth orbit before a lunar transfer
By being towed by another spacecraft
By a single direct burn straight to the Moon
questions.explanation: ISRO progressively raised the spacecraft's Earth orbit through several manoeuvres before sending it on a lunar transfer trajectory.

questions.q: Approximately how long was the Vikram lander and Pragyan rover designed to operate on the surface?
options: About one lunar day (around 14 Earth days)
questions.explanation: The surface mission was planned to last one lunar day, roughly 14 Earth days, the period of continuous sunlight at the site.

questions.q: Which earlier ISRO interplanetary success preceded Chandrayaan-2 and demonstrated India's deep-space capability?
questions.explanation: The Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan), which reached Mars in 2014, preceded Chandrayaan-2 and showcased ISRO's deep-space ability.

questions.q: Which follow-up mission later succeeded in soft-landing near the lunar south pole after Chandrayaan-2's lander failed?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 achieved a successful soft landing near the Moon's south pole in 2023, completing the goal Chandrayaan-2 had attempted.

questions.q: A scientific instrument on the Chandrayaan-2 orbiter detected and mapped what on the Moon?
options: Signatures of water/hydroxyl and surface minerals
questions.explanation: The orbiter's instruments mapped lunar surface composition, including water/hydroxyl signatures and minerals.
